title: Move Gemini API key from URL query string to HTTP header
description:
## Problem
`GeminiProvider.cs:44` places the `LlmApiKey` in the URL query string:
```csharp
var endpoint = $"/v1beta/models/{options.LlmApiKey}:generateContent?key={options.LlmApiKey}";
```
This leaks the API key to:
- Serilog HTTP request logging
- .NET HttpClient diagnostic handlers
- Proxy servers, CDNs, networking infrastructure

## Desired Outcome
API key passed via `x-goog-api-key` HTTP header instead of URL query string.

## Scope
- `Agent.Planning/Llm/GeminiProvider.cs` — change endpoint and add header
- Add unit test asserting the URL contains no `?key=`

## Non-Goals
- Changing any other provider (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic already use headers correctly)
- Adding general API key masking (covered by TSK-0184)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gemini API endpoint no longer includes `?key={apiKey}` in URL
- [ ] API key set via `request.Headers.Add("x-goog-api-key", ...)`
- [ ] Unit test asserts: URL does not contain API key; header does
- [ ] All existing tests pass
